title: Euodia roxburghiana
intro: Euodia roxburghiana, also known as Chinese angelica tree or Chinese prickly ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of China, India, and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities that can aid in the relief of pain, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as support the immune system. Euodia roxburghiana is rich in bioactive constituents, including lignans, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is prepared in various forms, including capsules, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in the production of herbal teas, tinctures, and essential oils.
